reforms.description: The Colombian government passed a major reform in 2011 to improve the allocation of royalties from natural resource exploitation, with a significant portion now allocated to an innovation fund aimed at boosting research and development projects. This reform aims to distribute revenues more equitably across regions and enhance investment effectiveness in science, technology, and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The allocation of royalties to innovation projects is expected to enhance productivity and investment in R&D, supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been in force since its passage in 2011.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in the allocation of public funds for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The recent reform of royalties aims at better distributing the resource revenues and promoting productivity growth in the non-commodity economy.

reforms.description: The Colombian government is working on a partnership with the South Korean Economic Development Co-operation Fund to invest in five new regional centres for innovation in information technology education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in regional centres for IT education is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, supporting innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The partnership is currently in the planning stages.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in research infrastructure but is not a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently working on a partnership with the South Korean Economic Development Co-operation Fund to invest in five new regional centres for innovation in information technology education.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Labour is creating a Public Employment Service, which includes implementing an information system on the labor market across the country and evaluating the performance of sectoral roundtables used by SENA to identify labor needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving labor market information and aligning skills with sectoral needs, the initiative aims to enhance the employability of the workforce, supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is currently in the planning stages.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve labor market alignment but does not constitute a major structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Labour is creating a Public Employment Service, the first step being the implementation of an information system on the labor market across the country.

reforms.description: Bancoldex, Colombia's largest public development bank, has shifted its focus towards SME financing and micro-lending, increasing the average maturity of loans to SMEs and providing public guarantees to facilitate access to credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to credit for SMEs, the reform is expected to support the growth of innovative firms and enhance economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The new financing focus has been put into practice.
reforms.importance_rationale: The shift in focus of a major public bank represents a significant change in the support for the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Bancoldex has strongly prioritised its SMEs financing and micro-lending business lines.
